units.title: Feelings & Emotions
units.subtitle: Happy, Sad, Angry, Sleepy, Hungry, Thirsty
grammar_focus: Yes, I am / No, I am not.

lessons.title: How Are You?
lessons.topic: Basic emotions and How are you?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What color is the boy's shirt in the video?
questions.hint: Look for the sun color on clothes!

questions.question_text: What action does the boy do when he is happy?
questions.hint: It's an action you do to make noise with your hands!

questions.question_text: Who feels happy in the song?
questions.hint: Look for the person with a big smile!

items.example: How are you? I am happy.
Are you sad? No, I am not.

steps.title: Amy and Tom's Day
steps.text: Hello, Amy! How do you feel? I am happy. How are you, Tom? I am sad. Where is my teddy bear?

steps.explanation: Use this to talk about how you feel.
examples: How are you? I am excited.
How are you? I am sleepy.
How are you? I am okay.

items.sentence: How am you? I am happy.
How are you? I is sad.

steps.audio_text: Hello! How are you today? I am happy because I found my yellow teddy bear.

questions.question: What color is the teddy bear?

questions.question_text: I ___ happy. (Grammar question)
Which word means 'feeling good'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: The question 'How are you?' asks about your feelings.
How are you? Choose the best answer. (Reading comprehension)

lessons.title: I Want Water
lessons.topic: Physical needs and Are you...? questions

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What does the boy want to drink?
questions.hint: It's clear and comes from the tap.

questions.question_text: Where is the boy feeling thirsty?
What should you do if you feel thirsty?
questions.hint: It makes your mouth less dry.

items.example: Are you hungry? Yes, I am.
How are you? I am thirsty.
Do you want to eat? Yes, I do.
Are you thirsty? Yes, I will drink.

steps.title: Park Adventure
steps.text: How are you? I am hungry. Are you thirsty? Yes, I am. Let's eat a sandwich and drink some juice. Are you happy now? Yes, I am!

steps.explanation: Use this to talk about feelings.
examples: Are you sleepy? Yes, I am.
Are you happy? Yes, I am.
Are you cold? No, I am not.

items.sentence: Are you thirsty? No, I ___ not.
Are you are happy? Yes, I am.

steps.audio_text: Are you hungry or thirsty? I am thirsty. Can I have some water, please?

questions.question: What does she want to drink?

questions.question_text: Are you ___? Yes, I am. (Grammar question)
Which one means 'drink'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: If you are thirsty, you need food.
What does the boy want in the story 'I Want Water'?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: How is the cat feeling after eating?
questions.hint: Think about how you feel after eating food.

questions.question_text: What do you do when you are tired?
What sound does a happy cat make?
questions.hint: It sounds like a soft engine.

items.example: Are you angry? Yes, I am.
How are you? I am sleepy.
Are you mad? No, I am not.
It is night. I am sleepy.

steps.title: Sleepy Night
steps.text: It is night. I am sleepy. My dog is sleepy too. Are you mad? No, I am not. I am happy.

steps.explanation: Ask and answer how you feel.
examples: Are you sleepy? Yes, I am.
Are you angry? No, I am not.
Are you happy? Yes, I am.

items.sentence: My face is ___. I am angry.
My face is ___. I am happy.
My face is red. I am feel angry.
My face are yellow. I am happy.

steps.audio_text: Are you sleepy, Anna? Yes, I am sleepy. It's night, and I'm ready for bed.

steps.prompt: Say: My face is red. I am mad.
steps.expected_text: my face is red. i am mad.

questions.question_text: My face is red. I am ___.
What does 'sleepy' mean?
True or False: Night is when it is very dark outside.
Fill in the blank: I am ___ when I want to go to bed.
Which color matches 'angry'?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you need when your throat is dry?
What happens when you run outside and it's very hot?
questions.hint: You need to drink after this.

questions.question_text: What can help when you're very thirsty?

steps.title: A Happy Day
steps.text: Hi, I am Lucy. How are you? I am happy because I am playing. Are you happy too? Yes, I am!

steps.audio_text: Hello! Are you happy today? I am happy because I have ice cream. Do you like ice cream too?

steps.prompt: Say: How are you feeling now and why?
steps.expected_text: i am happy because i am playing

questions.question_text: I ___ happy. (Grammar question)
Which emoji shows 'happy'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'Goodbye' is how you say hello.
Are you feeling hungry? (Reading comprehension from dialogue)
What do you say if you feel nice in the sun?
